questions.question: What is the main purpose of an introductory AI concepts unit?

options.label: A
options.text: To provide a high-level foundation for understanding what AI can do

options.label: B
options.text: To teach advanced machine learning mathematics

options.label: D
options.text: To certify a learner as an expert AI engineer

questions.correctAnswer: To provide a high-level foundation for understanding what AI can do
correctAnswers: To provide a high-level foundation for understanding what AI can do

options.label: A
options.text: A broad set of capabilities that help systems perform tasks associated with human-like reasoning, perception, or language

options.label: B
options.text: A single database product used only for storing text

options.label: C
options.text: A hardware component found only in servers

options.label: D
options.text: A replacement for every software application

questions.correctAnswer: A broad set of capabilities that help systems perform tasks associated with human-like reasoning, perception, or language
correctAnswers: A broad set of capabilities that help systems perform tasks associated with human-like reasoning, perception, or language
questions.rationale: AI covers many capabilities, including language, speech, vision, generation, and decision support.

questions.question: What activity happens during the pre-processing stage of speech recognition?

options.label: A
options.text: The final transcript is capitalized and punctuated.

options.label: B
options.text: Feature vectors are extracted from the audio waveform for modeling.

options.label: C
options.text: The text is converted into spoken audio.

options.label: D
options.text: The decoder selects the final word sequence.

questions.correctAnswer: Feature vectors are extracted from the audio waveform for modeling.
correctAnswers: Feature vectors are extracted from the audio waveform for modeling.
questions.rationale: Pre-processing transforms raw audio into compact feature vectors, commonly using MFCCs, so later models can recognize speech patterns.

questions.question: Why is language modeling needed after acoustic modeling?

options.label: B

options.label: C
options.text: Language modeling removes hums and clicks from the input signal.

options.label: D
options.text: Language modeling converts normalized text into audio.

questions.correctAnswer: Phoneme predictions alone may be ambiguous, so vocabulary and grammar help choose likely word sequences.
correctAnswers: Phoneme predictions alone may be ambiguous, so vocabulary and grammar help choose likely word sequences.
questions.rationale: Language models use vocabulary, grammar, context, and domain patterns to resolve ambiguous sound sequences such as "their" and "there."

questions.question: What does beam search do during decoding?

options.label: A
options.text: It keeps a shortlist of likely partial transcriptions and prunes weaker paths.

options.label: B
options.text: It expands abbreviations such as "Dr." into "Doctor."

options.label: C
options.text: It converts speech audio into mel-spectrograms.

questions.correctAnswer: It keeps a shortlist of likely partial transcriptions and prunes weaker paths.
correctAnswers: It keeps a shortlist of likely partial transcriptions and prunes weaker paths.

options.label: B

options.label: C
options.text: Prosody generation predicts pitch, duration, and emphasis.

questions.correctAnswer: Text normalization expands raw text into spoken forms.; Linguistic analysis maps text to phonemes.; Prosody generation predicts pitch, duration, and emphasis.
correctAnswers: Text normalization expands raw text into spoken forms.
